violations.text: Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Chicken was observed stored above pork. Items relocated. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 100 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called dish machine company at the time of inspection to adjust dish washer. Technician stated that they will service unit tomorrow morning. All dishes/utensils to go through 3-bay sink sanitizer until unit is serviced and tested to be adequate ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2025

The nonfood contact surfaces of the following was observed with an accumulation of debris: -Nonfood contact surfaces of the fan cover of the prep and reach-in units. -Nonfood contact surfaces of the shelving in the walk-in and cooling rack in the kitchen. -Nonfood contact surfaces of the cover for the vent above the dish washer. Areas to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2025

The pre-flush spray hose at the dish machine is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2025

A small amount of old rodent droppings were observed behind various equipment in the basement area. Establishment to pull equipment away from walls and clean and sanitize area. Area to be monitored to determine if there is new activity. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2025

Foods in the walk-in were observed held at 43 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Unit was adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

Various foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

Utensils were stored between prep unit/tables. PIC removed utensils. Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC placed in hot water. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of bio-film. PIC to wash rinse and sanitize ice machine by end of day. Shelving next to the cook line/prep unit has an accumulation of grease and debris. Shelving/prep unit to be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

The pre-flush spray hose at the dish machine is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2024

The dumpster and grease disposal outside is located on dirt. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2024
